blocks.content: Her hand on his. The kitchen quiet. Upstairs, a door that wasn't quite closed.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You said the label doesn't change what it is. So ask me. Give it the label.
He turned his hand over under hers so their palms met.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Daisy Jones. Will you be my girlfriend.

She looked at him for a long moment — the full look, checking if something was real — and then something in her face settled. Not surprised. Relieved. Like a breath held for a long time finally let out.
He laughed — not a small one, a real one — and she laughed with him, and it was the most natural thing in the world and also the end of something that had been almost for seven years.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You've been asking since we were twelve. Just not out loud.

She was still smiling — the private one, the one that was only ever his — and he was still holding her hand and the kitchen was warm and the whole house was quiet and the December outside had gone cold and still.
They stayed at that table for another hour. Talking, not about the distance or the future or spring semester, just — talking. The way they always had. Like nothing had changed.
